metadata.title: Staging/Prod/Remote Test Suite Tier Redesign
metadata.rationale:
The current 3-tier test system (staging/prod/gpu) does not account for tests that require running Docker Compose services (MLflow, MinIO, Prefect, Dashboard, MONAILabel) or remote cloud infrastructure. These tests currently FAIL with confusing errors instead of being cleanly separated into their own tier.
Additionally, SAM3 model loading tests were incorrectly placed in tests/v2/integration/ instead of tests/gpu_instance/, wasting 45+ minutes per prod test run on OOM/timeout failures.

current-state.description: Current test tiers do not cleanly separate by infrastructure requirements. Tests that need Docker Compose services are mixed with unit tests in tests/v2/integration/, causing failures when services aren't running.

tiers.tier: Excludes: model_loading, slow, integration markers. Target: <3 min. Works well.
Includes: everything except gpu_instance/. Problem: includes Docker Compose service tests that fail without stack. Target: ~5-10 min. Currently 10+ min with many failures.

category.name: Docker Compose services required
category.description: These tests need running Docker Compose services (MLflow, MinIO, Prefect, Dashboard, MONAILabel). They are infrastructure validation tests, not code regression tests. They should run when the developer has the stack up (docker compose up), not during make test-prod.

category.name: Real mlruns data required
category.description: These tests need actual training results in mlruns/. They validate the MLflow-as-contract between flows. They should run after a training run completes, not during make test-prod.

category.name: Cloud/remote infrastructure
category.description: Already separated into tests/v2/cloud/ with auto-skip. Works well.

category.name: GPU instance validation (SAM3)
category.description: SAM3 model loading tests MOVED from tests/v2/integration/ to tests/gpu_instance/. Run once per new GPU instance type (L4, A100) via make test-gpu. NEVER in staging or prod tiers. BANNED from CI.

proposed-tiers.description: 5-tier test architecture that cleanly separates by infrastructure requirements. Each tier has a clear "when to run" and "what you need" contract.
proposed-tiers.tier: Pure unit tests + config validation. No models, no services, no data. Target: <3 min. Already works. Marker exclusions: not model_loading, not slow, not integration

All code tests including model_loading (DynUNet, MambaVesselNet) but EXCLUDING tests that need Docker Compose services or real mlruns data. Target: ~5 min. New marker exclusion: not requires_services, not requires_mlruns
Tests that validate Docker Compose service health, inter-service contracts, and the full e2e pipeline. Run when developer has the stack up. Marker: @pytest.mark.requires_services New Makefile target.
Tests that validate MLflow artifacts, checkpoints, metric integrity. Run after a training run completes (debug or production). Marker: @pytest.mark.requires_mlruns New Makefile target.
SAM3 model loading, forward passes, ONNX export. Run once per new GPU instance type. Already works via tests/gpu_instance/.

implementation-tasks.task: Add @pytest.mark.requires_services to all Docker Compose service tests
Add @pytest.mark.requires_mlruns to all real mlruns data tests
Update make test-prod to exclude requires_services and requires_mlruns
Add make test-stack target that runs only requires_services tests
Add make test-post-training target that runs only requires_mlruns tests
Update tests/CLAUDE.md with new tier documentation
Remove VRAM guard skip logic from moved SAM3 tests (not needed in gpu_instance/)

immediate-actions.action: Docker Compose service tests have skip guards for now (temporary — proper tier split is T1-T7)
Real mlruns tests have skip guards for now (temporary — proper tier split is T2-T7)
